# n-dx

> Codebase analysis generated by Sourcevision.
> When using this document: group related items together and separate unrelated concerns.

## Project Overview

- **Files**: 1028
- **Lines of code**: 344,508
- **Languages**: TypeScript (868), CSS (44), Markdown (32), JavaScript (31), JSON (30)
- **Zones**: 35 (35 with descriptions)
- **Git**: feature/integrate-codex @ 21786d2
- **Analyzed**: 2026-03-06T15:09:46.541Z

## Architecture Zones

### Autonomous Agent (`autonomous-agent`)

The hench package — a self-contained autonomous agent that picks PRD tasks, builds execution briefs, runs Claude API tool-use loops, and records run history.

- Files: 159
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/autonomous-agent/context.md`

### Design Documentation (`design-documentation`)

Architecture decision records, memory system design notes, and PRD steward vision documents that capture the project's evolving technical design.

- Files: 16
- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/design-documentation/context.md`

### DOM Performance Monitoring (`dom-performance-monitoring`)

Encapsulates runtime DOM performance measurement, pairing a low-level monitor utility with a React hook that surfaces metrics to the viewer.

- Files: 3
- Cohesion: 0.75, Coupling: 0.25
- Detail: `.sourcevision/zones/dom-performance-monitoring/context.md`

- Cohesion: 1.00, Coupling: 0.00

## Route Structure

### Server API Routes

**/api/hench/adaptive/** (handleAdaptiveRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-adaptive.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/adaptive/override/:key
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/analysis
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/history
GET     /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/apply
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/dismiss/:id
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/lock/:key
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/override
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/settings
POST    /api/hench/adaptive/unlock/:key
```

**/api/ndx-config** (handleConfigRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-config.ts`

```
GET     /api/ndx-config
GET     /api/projects
POST    /api/projects/switch
```

**/api/features** (handleFeaturesRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-features.ts`

```
GET     /api/features
PUT     /api/features
```

**/api/hench/** (handleHenchRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-hench.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/audit
GET     /api/hench/concurrency
GET     /api/hench/config
GET     /api/hench/execute/status
GET     /api/hench/execute/status/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory
GET     /api/hench/memory/history
GET     /api/hench/memory/history/:taskId
GET     /api/hench/memory/leaks
GET     /api/hench/metrics
GET     /api/hench/metrics/snapshots
GET     /api/hench/runs
GET     /api/hench/runs/:id
GET     /api/hench/runs/health
GET     /api/hench/task-usage
GET     /api/hench/templates
GET     /api/hench/templates/:id
GET     /api/hench/throttle
POST    /api/hench/execute
POST    /api/hench/execute/:taskId/terminate
POST    /api/hench/runs/:id/mark-stuck
POST    /api/hench/templates
POST    /api/hench/templates/:id/apply
POST    /api/hench/throttle/emergency-stop
POST    /api/hench/throttle/pause
POST    /api/hench/throttle/resume
PUT     /api/hench/config
PUT     /api/hench/throttle
```

**/api/** (handleIntegrationRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-integrations.ts`

```
DELETE  /api/integrations/:id/config

```

```
GET     /api/notion/config
POST    /api/notion/schema
POST    /api/notion/schema/fix
POST    /api/notion/test
PUT     /api/notion/config
```

```

```

**/api/rex/** (handleRexRoute) — `packages/web/src/server/routes-rex.ts`

```

DELETE  /api/rex/items/:id/requirements/:rid
```

```
POST    /api/hench/workflow/apply
POST    /api/hench/workflow/suggestions/:id
```

### Components: 5 total, 0 usage edges

## Findings

### Anti-patterns

- With only 2 production files (hook + utility), this zone is below the minimum viable zone size for independent lifecycle management; if no additional performance-monitoring files are planned, merging these two files into the web-viewer zone as an internal module would reduce zone overhead without losing the clean separation of concerns. [info] (dom-performance-monitoring)
- There is no test file covering the `ndx sync` command (sync-with-remote adapter path) visible in the listed files, despite sync being a documented ndx orchestration command; if this gap exists, the CLI delegation contract for sync is unverified at the E2E layer. [warning] (orchestrator-e2e-tests)
- analysis.ts is co-located in prd-input-panels but imports BrandedHeader from web-dashboard-core (logos.ts), creating the prd-input-panels → web-dashboard-core coupling direction. BrandedHeader is a pure presentational component with no domain logic; it should be extracted into a shared UI primitives location accessible to all viewer zones without creating cross-zone coupling. [warning] (prd-input-panels)
- usage-cleanup-scheduler.ts documents that it 'Broadcasts a WebSocket event so the dashboard can react' but does not import websocket infrastructure directly — the WebSocket dependency is injected at the call site, hiding a runtime coupling that the static zone graph cannot measure. This invisible dependency means the zone's coupling score of 0.38 understates its true runtime coupling. [warning] (task-usage-tracking)
- physics.ts is classified as part of the 'web-unit' sub-zone due to Louvain co-placement with its test files, but it is a pure production module (Barnes-Hut quad-tree engine extracted from graph.ts). This misclassification generates a false 'production-to-test' coupling alarm; relocate physics.ts adjacent to graph.ts in the viewer/graph/ directory to align its zone membership with its actual consumers. [warning] (web-dashboard-core)
- elapsed-time.ts and task-audit.ts are misplaced in the scaffold zone but are primarily consumed by web-viewer; they should be co-located with their consumers to eliminate the layering inversion these files cause. [warning] (web-package-scaffold)
- web-package-scaffold imports from web-viewer (4 edges), inverting the expected config-layer→application-layer dependency direction; bootstrap and config files must not depend on the application zone they initialize. [critical] (web-package-scaffold)
- boundary-check.test.ts enforces a package-wide server/viewer import boundary for all of src/ but is grouped into the websocket-infrastructure zone, making it appear to be a WebSocket contract test. This misplacement means the boundary enforcement test is invisible when reviewing the web package's overall test strategy and would not be found by developers looking for architectural guard tests. [warning] (websocket-infrastructure)
- God function: main in packages/rex/src/cli/index.ts calls 48 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]
- God function: usePRDActions in packages/web/src/viewer/hooks/use-prd-actions.ts calls 39 unique functions — consider decomposing into smaller, focused functions [warning]

### Patterns

- The e2e suite covers CLI delegation but not HTTP/MCP session lifecycle; as the web package's HTTP transport matures, a separate e2e zone targeting the live server would close this coverage gap without violating the zero-coupling constraint of the existing orchestrator suite. [warning] (orchestrator-e2e-tests)
- analysis.ts (page/view) co-located with panel components inside the same zone blends routing and component responsibilities; extracting the view into web-dashboard-core or a dedicated views zone would likely eliminate one direction of the bidirectional coupling with the core. [warning] (prd-input-panels)
- Coupling-to-cohesion ratio of ~0.60 is the highest in this batch — external pull on this zone is nearly as strong as its internal coherence, signaling an unstable boundary. [warning] (task-usage-tracking)
- Zero coupling in both directions (no imports to or from any zone) is the expected and correct pattern for an HTML bootstrap shell — no action needed and serves as a reference for static asset zone hygiene. [info] (viewer-shell)

### Relationships

- dom-performance-monitoring → web-viewer is the only cross-zone edge for this zone; if performance monitoring ever needs to instrument zones other than web-viewer (e.g. web-server), a shared utility layer would be required to avoid duplicating measurement logic. [info] (dom-performance-monitoring)
- web-viewer imports from web-unit 6 times — the largest single cross-zone edge in the graph. If web-unit is a test-support zone, this is a critical production→test boundary violation. [critical] (web-dashboard-core)
- web-viewer → web (2 imports) pulls scaffold or config-layer code into the viewer at runtime; confirm these are not test utilities or startup-only helpers leaking into the production module graph. [warning] (web-dashboard-core)
- web-viewer → web (2 imports) combined with web → web-viewer (4 imports) means the scaffold zone both consumes and is consumed by the viewer core — a layering inversion where the entry/config layer depends on the application layer and vice versa. [warning] (web-package-scaffold)
- Purely unidirectional dependency flow (consumed by web-dashboard-core, emits nothing outward) — reference pattern for how satellite zones should relate to the core zone. [info] (websocket-infrastructure)
- Tightly coupled modules: packages/web/src/server/routes-rex.ts and packages/web/src/server/types.ts — 142 cross-file calls (unidirectional). Consider extracting shared interface or merging [info]

### Observations

- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (autonomous-agent)
- At 159 files in a single zone, hench may benefit from internal zone decomposition (e.g. separating agent core, tool dispatch, and run storage) to make internal cohesion visible in future sourcevision analyses. [info] (autonomous-agent)
- The agent/analysis/ pipeline (adaptive, review, spin) represents complex stateful LLM orchestration; each phase should have deterministic unit tests using mocked LLM responses to avoid flaky integration test suites. [info] (autonomous-agent)
- Zero coupling confirms the rex-gateway pattern is working correctly; all rex imports are concentrated in a single file, making the dependency surface explicit and auditable. [info] (autonomous-agent)
- High cohesion (1) — files are tightly interconnected [info] (design-documentation)
- Design docs appear disconnected from the PRD task tree; linking doc files from the relevant rex tasks (or vice versa) would prevent design intent from diverging from implementation plans. [info] (design-documentation)
- The 2026-03-03 refresh analysis file uses a date prefix, suggesting ad-hoc session notes rather than stable reference docs; a clear distinction between living reference docs and ephemeral analysis notes would help long-term maintainability. [info] (design-documentation)
- The memory subsystem is documented across four separate files; consolidating them under a docs/memory/ subdirectory would improve navigability without losing any content. [info] (design-documentation)
- Bidirectional coupling: "web-dashboard-core" ↔ "web-package-scaffold" (2+4 crossings) — consider extracting shared interface [warning]
- Size imbalance: largest zone has 340 files vs smallest with 3 — uneven decomposition [info]
- ... and 32 more

### Suggestions

- Zone "autonomous-agent" has files across 31 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory [info] (autonomous-agent)
- Rename zone 'autonomous-agent' to 'hench-agent' or 'hench-core' to align with the implicit {package}-{role} naming convention used by all other package-level zones (web-viewer, web-server, rex-runtime-state); the function-based name makes it impossible to locate hench's zone by scanning for the package name. [info] (autonomous-agent)
- The rex-gateway.ts pattern provides type-level protection at the import boundary, but zero external coupling means no coupling metric will ever warn if hench's internal contracts drift from rex's exported types — add a CI step that runs tsc --noEmit across the hench→rex gateway interface specifically, ensuring type drift is caught before runtime rather than only during full builds. [warning] (autonomous-agent)
- packages/web/tests/unit/viewer/ contains files from 5 zones (autonomous-agent, dom-performance-monitoring, prd-input-panels, web-dashboard-core, web-package-scaffold) — consider grouping into subdirectories to clarify architectural boundaries [info]
- Add a CI gate that fails if any new bidirectional import pair is introduced in the web package's zone graph. The three existing bidirectional pairs (web-viewer↔web-server, web-viewer↔panel, web-viewer↔web-package-scaffold) are known debt; a CI gate would prevent the pattern from expanding while the existing cases are resolved incrementally. [warning]
- Adopt a zone naming policy that encodes role rather than structure: prefer 'spa-bootstrap' over 'viewer-shell', 'prd-authoring' over 'prd-input-panels', 'web-runtime' over 'web-dashboard-core'. Zone names chosen at Louvain detection time tend to reflect topology rather than intent and should be reviewed whenever a zone's responsibilities are clarified. [info]
- Create a shared-ui leaf zone (e.g., packages/web/src/viewer/shared-ui/) for purely presentational primitives like BrandedHeader/logos.ts; this single change would eliminate at least one direction of the prd-input-panels↔web-dashboard-core bidirectional coupling and provide a stable, convention-backed location for future shared components. [warning]
- Establish a documented convention distinguishing unit, integration, and architecture-guard tests by directory tier (tests/unit/, tests/integration/, tests/architecture/); boundary-check.test.ts and ws-health-integration.test.ts are both currently misplaced under tests/unit/server/, creating a systematic misclassification that affects zone coupling scores and CI tier filtering. [warning]
- Establish and document a canonical zone naming convention (e.g. `{package}-{role}`) and apply it retroactively to 'autonomous-agent' (→ 'hench-agent') and any other zones using non-standard naming patterns; this makes the zone map self-documenting and allows engineers to identify a zone's owning package by name alone. [info]
- Introduce a zone-type annotation convention (e.g. a 'role' field in zone metadata) that distinguishes: production-leaf, production-hub, test-e2e, test-unit, architecture-enforcement, artifact. This would immediately resolve the recurring misclassification of orchestrator-e2e-tests (cohesion 1.0 as topological artifact), dom-performance-monitoring (micro production-leaf), and .rex/docs (data artifact zones) — all of which produce misleading health scores under the current single-metric system. [info]
- ... and 31 more

## Tooling

This project uses n-dx for codebase analysis (Sourcevision) and requirement
tracking (Rex). MCP tools are available for interactive access to analysis
data and PRD management. Skills available: /plan, /status, /capture,
/zone, /work, /configure.
